Patch Set 1:

I'm totally fine with this change in principle, but I think it is too big and changing too many things to be easily landed.  Trying to convert every lnet/ typedef in a single patch just makes the whole thing too large and very likely to hit conflicts just because the patch is so large.

What makes patches like this more easily created and landed without conflicts is to add the struct name to all of the typedef'd structs, but NOT remove the typedef in the same patch.  The typedefs should be marked deprecated in checkpatch.pl in the first patch so that new patches do not add more of them (though there may be some patches in flight that are still using them).

Then, a later patch can go through and remove the typedefs a few weeks later, both at the struct definition as well as in the code where any patches that were landed while this one was being reviewed.

That avoids a flag day that breaks any in-flight patches, and also allows this huge patch to be split into smaller chunks since the original typedef is not being removed.
Uploaded patch set 2.

This is a 10k lines patch. Could you split it into more manageable parts? I spent 10 minutes on it and got to only a fifth.

Patch Set 2:

James,

Maybe I misunderstood Andreas, but did you make the change he requested?  It doesn't look like it to me, and the patch is just *enormous*.  I shudder at the idea of trying to review it properly. (and "it builds" isn't enough of a review - What if you accidentally swapped one struct for another?  Not saying you did, but I have to *review* for that.)

Even if I misunderstood and you have made the requested change, I think Frank is right, it's too large.

Patch Set 2:

Oh I missed Andreas comment until after I submitted the second version which was a build fix. The main reason for this patch is to ensure what I will be pushing upstream doesn't  break anything and it is helpful to have more eyes. This patch wasn't intended really for the OpenSFS branch. Well I will be pushing the change that impacts userspace tools to ensure our utilities can build against upstream. So this patch will be updated.

The reality is with multi-rail coming and the vast amounts of lnet style issues which if this was all addressed in master would take over a year to complete. I expect LNet upstream might be out of staging before then. Is it really worth it cleanup LNet in master?

Patch Set 2:

Also I made sure this didn't break DVS :-)

Don't know if another vote is needed, but:

Different struct types strikes me as a low effort thing that could save us from some typo-like "easy error to make, hard error to see" problems.  I mean, that's sort of what compiler type checking is for.  To take that burden off us.

parents.subject: LU-9040 scrub: handle group boundary properly

commit.subject: LU-6142 lnet: remove most of typedefs from LNet headers
commit.message:
LU-6142 lnet: remove most of typedefs from LNet headers

Remove the majority of typedefs from the LNet headers.
Change them into structures instead. Currently only
lnet_nid_t, lnet_pid_t, and lnet_kiov_t are left.

Test-Parameters: trivial
